# Pro Se Bankruptcy - File Without a Lawyer [2026 Guide]
> Free Pro Se bankruptcy guide for 2026. Compare Chapter 7 vs Chapter 13, local exemptions, filing costs, means test, and what to expect in your case.

## About
prosebankruptcy.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Bankruptcy Resources - Accessible for All | Deaf, Blind & Disability Guides [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/accessibility.html
- Bankruptcy Resources for Blind & Visually Impaired [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/blind-bankruptcy.html
- Common Mistakes with Pro Se Bankruptcy | Pro Se Bankruptcy: https://prosebankruptcy.org/common-mistakes.html
- Filing Bankruptcy as a Deaf Person - Your Rights & Resources [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/deaf-bankruptcy.html
- Frequently Asked Questions About Pro Se Bankruptcy | Pro Se Bankruptcy: https://prosebankruptcy.org/faq.html
- Pro Se Bankruptcy FAQ - Filing Without a Lawyer: https://prosebankruptcy.org/frequently-asked-questions.html
- Pro Se Bankruptcy - File Without a Lawyer: https://prosebankruptcy.org/
- Military Bankruptcy FAQ - 12 Questions Answered [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/military-bankruptcy-faq.html
- Military Bankruptcy - SCRA Protections and Active Duty Filing [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/military-bankruptcy.html
- Pro Se Bankruptcy in Missouri | Pro Se Bankruptcy: https://prosebankruptcy.org/missouri-guide.html
- Pro Se Bankruptcy Filing Checklist - Step-by-Step Without a Lawyer: https://prosebankruptcy.org/pro-se-checklist.html
- Security Clearance and Bankruptcy - Section 525, DoD Guidelines [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/security-clearance-bankruptcy.html
- Veteran Bankruptcy - VA Benefits, Disability Exemptions [2026] - Pro Se: https://prosebankruptcy.org/veteran-bankruptcy.html
- Privacy Policy: https://prosebankruptcy.org/privacy.html

## Contact
- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
